title: Switch reconciles a native identity: register config.substrate_id -> dir when absent; mismatch still errors
context: FGAP-003: neither switch path records the active substrate's identity, so a valid switch to an unregistered substrate false-fails context-validate with substrate_id_unregistered.
decision: All three switch engine functions — switchToExisting, switchAndCreate, AND switchToPrevious — register config.substrate_id -> active dir when the id is ABSENT from the project-root registry, via a shared reconcileActiveSubstrateRegistration(cwd) helper called after the pointer flip. Register-if-absent is functionally safe: an absent id cannot collide, so registering it in this project's local namespace is harmless; a present-but-different-dir entry is the substrate_id_registry_mismatch case and is LEFT untouched so genuine drift still surfaces. The helper is best-effort: a config that cannot load/validate is caught and skipped so a switch never breaks (a user may switch TO a substrate to repair it); a pre-identity substrate (no substrate_id) is skipped. This decision records only NATIVE re-registration; origin/provenance for imported (foreign) substrates is out of scope here and handled by the provenance ledger in DEC-0002 — a foreign substrate is brought in via clone, not silently absorbed by switch. DEC-0003 is the native-only case of DEC-0002's model.
consequences: All three switch engine functions (switchToExisting, switchAndCreate, switchToPrevious) gain a register-if-absent step after the pointer flip, via the shared reconcileActiveSubstrateRegistration helper.
Eliminates the false substrate_id_unregistered on a valid switch, switch -c, or switch - .
Does NOT record origin/provenance — that enrichment lands with DEC-0002; until then a switched-in id is registered without lineage.
Best-effort: an unloadable/invalid target config is skipped (switch still flips); context-validate surfaces the config problem separately.

Tests: switch / switch-c / switch- to an unregistered-but-config-bearing substrate -> registered + context-validate clean; a wrong-dir entry -> substrate_id_registry_mismatch preserved; pre-identity + invalid-config -> no-op skip.
